description_long: This object is a delicate flower with slender, elongated petals that are predominantly pale purple with hints of white and pink. The petals are thin and slightly curved, radiating outward from a central point, giving the flower a wispy, airy appearance. The flower head sits atop a sturdy green stem, which is straight and supports the bloom above a cluster of broad, lanceolate green leaves. The leaves are arranged in a whorled fashion around the lower part of the stem, providing a strong base for the flower. The overall structure is upright and elegant, with the flower positioned at the terminal end of the stem. The coloration and form suggest a wildflower commonly found in urban parks or roadside green spaces.
description: A slender, pale purple flower with elongated petals radiating from a central point, supported by a green stem and broad lanceolate leaves at the base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full bloom with petals radiating outward and the green leaves clearly visible at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thinness of the petals and the upright stem, with leaves partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the flower head and the arrangement of the leaves around the stem.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the curved petals and the whorled leaf arrangement at the stem's base.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, radiating symmetrically from the center of the flower head with slight overlapping and a whorled appearance.
